acceptance criteria: Notebook executes with nbclient, artifacts exist and are nonblank, final JSON contains incidence/transform/finite-plane checks, and no generic renderer scaffold remains.
chapter goal: Use homogeneous coordinates to compute RP^2, infinity, joins/meets, duality, projective maps, and finite projective planes.
computational checks: finite representatives project to w=1; infinity representatives have w=0
chart coordinates reconstruct projectively equivalent representatives
limit residual decreases and final coordinate tends to zero
cross products are orthogonal to both inputs
constructed lines pass through the center and the selected infinity points
incidence counts agree after swapping roles
point images match four targets and line action preserves incidence
counts and uniqueness axioms hold modulo q
concept inventory: one-dimensional subspaces of K^3 as projective points
two-dimensional subspaces represented by line normals
affine chart w=1 and other chart choices
join and meet as cross products
parallel construction relative to a chosen infinity line
projective transformations and inverse-transpose line action
finite projective planes over GF(2) and GF(3)
implementation notes: All artifacts are generated by this canonical notebook under the chapter-03 artifact subtree; no shared utilities or indexes are modified.

library routing table.inspection_target: Finite rays hit the chart; infinity rays lie in w=0 and do not hit it.
library routing table.representation: 3D rays meeting the w=1 affine chart
library routing table.validation: finite representatives project to w=1; infinity representatives have w=0

library routing table.inspection_target: A point invisible in the w-chart can be visible in another chart.
library routing table.representation: two affine charts of the same projective classes
library routing table.validation: chart coordinates reconstruct projectively equivalent representatives

library routing table.inspection_target: The finite location diverges while the scaled homogeneous vector converges.
library routing table.representation: limit of p + alpha r under homogeneous rescaling
library routing table.validation: limit residual decreases and final coordinate tends to zero

library routing table.concept: Joins and meets via cross product
library routing table.inspection_target: The same meet formula returns a finite point or a point at infinity.
library routing table.representation: ordinary and parallel intersections computed by cross products
library routing table.validation: cross products are orthogonal to both inputs

library routing table.concept: Parallelism after choosing a line at infinity
library routing table.inspection_target: Changing the infinity line changes what the word parallel means.
library routing table.representation: quadrangle construction with a finite line declared to be infinity
library routing table.validation: constructed lines pass through the center and the selected infinity points

library routing table.inspection_target: Six joins of four points dualize to six meets of four lines.
library routing table.representation: complete quadrangle and coordinate-dual line arrangement
library routing table.validation: incidence counts agree after swapping roles

library routing table.inspection_target: Lines remain lines while distances, angles, and circles need not survive.
library routing table.representation: interactive grid and circle image under a four-corner homography
library routing table.validation: point images match four targets and line action preserves incidence

library routing table.inspection_target: Every row and column has q+1 hits; any two points determine one line.
library routing table.representation: incidence matrices for GF(2) and GF(3)
library routing table.validation: counts and uniqueness axioms hold modulo q

proof-visualization strategy: Use invariant trackers: dot-product incidence, cross-product orthogonality, inverse-transpose line action, and finite-model axiom checks.
risks: Projective classes are scale classes, so numeric comparisons use incidence residuals or normalized representatives rather than raw coordinate equality.
source span read: Chapter 3, sections 3.1-3.7, printed pp. 47-66 / PDF pp. 69-88.
